title: The Potato Eaters
content:
The potato eaters -
        grim, weathered souls
        wrenching a meal
        from sandy waste.

        The dark toil lined ridges
        carried from their fields
        to each human face,
        dim, pale light
        as shadowy as
        lives eked out upon this stoney rash of soil.

        Brows, a murky legend
        of overwork -
        deflected hope,
        seasons up in the smoke
        of a potato boil.
meaning:
Paul Cameron Brown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Potato Eaters"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
